title: Market Brief — Sunday, May 31, 2026
summary: Compute markets are splitting hard. H200 is in severe shortage at 8% available capacity, H100 is tight at 14%, while B200 sits in oversupply at 51% as new silicon floods in faster than demand absorbs it — utilization crashed 41.5 percentage points in 30 days. The headline shock: H100 spot plunged 36.8% overnight to $2.81/hr, signaling either a demand cliff or a liquidation event against a backdrop of 82.3% annualized volatility.
paragraphs: Compute markets are splitting hard. H200 is in severe shortage at 8% available capacity, H100 is tight at 14%, while B200 sits in oversupply at 51% as new silicon floods in faster than demand absorbs it — utilization crashed 41.5 percentage points in 30 days. The headline shock: H100 spot plunged 36.8% overnight to $2.81/hr, signaling either a demand cliff or a liquidation event against a backdrop of 82.3% annualized volatility.

At TCO-adjusted cost (1.25x spot), B200 inference runs $5.85/M tokens for Llama 70B, making it the only GPU where medium-tier API sellers break even. DeepSeek at $0.45/M output and Meta Llama at $0.32/M are selling well below any TCO floor — Burning Cash. Anthropic charges $25/M for Opus 4.6 against sub-$10 production cost — Strong. OpenAI's sprawling lineup from $0.18 to $80/M output is incoherent, blending premium margin on o3 Pro with loss-leader gpt-oss plays — Neutral. Google — Neutral. DeepSeek — Burning Cash.

B200 at 51% availability and $6.40/hr spot is overvalued relative to collapsing utilization and should be shorted against forward contracts. H200 at 8% availability and only 2.3% daily price decline is undervalued given the tightest supply in the stack — accumulate H200 capacity commitments now before scarcity reprices upward.
bullets: 8% available H200 capacity marks the most severe compute shortage across all GPU classes.
51% B200 availability confirms oversupply after utilization dropped 41.5 percentage points in 30 days.
$2.81/hr H100 spot after a 36.8% single-day crash represents the largest overnight move in the dataset.
21.78 LLMflation index means frontier output tokens cost 78% less than GPT-4 launch pricing.
$0.18/M output from OpenAI gpt-oss-120b undercuts TCO-adjusted Llama 70B cost by roughly $5.67/M tokens.
192.3% annualized volatility on H200 OCPI makes it the highest-risk GPU contract to hold unhedged.
